key_topics: Operations and Algebraic Thinking: writing and interpreting numerical expressions
Order of operations (parentheses, brackets, braces)
Generating two patterns from two rules and identifying relationships
Number and Operations in Base Ten: place value to billions and thousandths
Rounding decimals to any place
Multiplying multi-digit whole numbers fluently
Dividing with two-digit divisors
Adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing decimals to hundredths
Number and Operations — Fractions: adding and subtracting fractions with unlike denominators
Multiplying fractions and mixed numbers
Dividing unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions
Fractions as division
Measurement and Data: converting measurement units (same system)
Volume: understanding volume, using cubic units
Volume of rectangular prisms using V = l × w × h and V = b × h
Line plots with fractional data
Geometry: coordinate plane in the first quadrant
Classifying two-dimensional figures into categories
competences: Apply order of operations with parentheses and brackets
Fluently multiply multi-digit numbers and divide with two-digit divisors
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions including mixed numbers
Add and subtract decimals to hundredths fluently
Calculate volume of rectangular prisms using formulas
typical_mistakes: Ignoring order of operations (left-to-right instead of PEMDAS)
Adding both numerators and denominators when adding fractions with unlike denominators
Multiplying denominators instead of finding LCM
Placing decimal points incorrectly when multiplying decimals

key_topics: Reading Literature: theme and character development across chapters
Comparing and contrasting two or more stories in the same genre
Analyzing how narrator's or speaker's point of view influences descriptions
Reading Informational Text: explaining relationships among concepts
Drawing on information from multiple texts
Analyzing how an author uses evidence to support points
Greek and Latin affixes and roots
Reading grade-level text with fluency and comprehension
Writing: opinion essays with organized arguments and evidence
Informative writing: organized paragraphs with domain-specific vocabulary
Narrative writing: establishing situation, characters, problem, resolution
Research: conducting short research projects, synthesizing sources
Language: conjunctions, prepositions, interjections, perfect verb tenses, correlative conjunctions
competences: Write multi-paragraph opinion essays with evidence-based reasons
Analyze author's purpose and point of view
Synthesize information from multiple texts on the same topic
Demonstrate command of grade 5 grammar and language conventions
typical_mistakes: Confusing author's purpose with author's point of view
Not distinguishing fact from opinion in texts
Weak essay conclusions (just restating the thesis)
Overusing simple sentences (lack of sentence variety)
Errors with perfect tenses (has gone vs. went)

key_topics: Matter and its interactions: properties of matter, mixtures and solutions
Chemical reactions: evidence that a chemical reaction has occurred
Ecosystems: food webs, cycling of matter, flow of energy
Biodiversity and humans: human impact on ecosystems
Earth's systems: water cycle, wind and ocean patterns
Earth's history: rock strata, geologic time
Space systems: patterns of stars, role of the sun
competences: Trace matter and energy through ecosystems
Analyze human impacts on Earth's systems
Describe patterns of Earth's orbit and their effects
typical_mistakes: Thinking decomposers are not part of the food web

key_topics: The Civil War: causes (slavery, states' rights), major battles, Emancipation Proclamation
Reconstruction: successes and failures, effects on freed slaves
Immigration and industrialization (late 1800s)
Progressive Era: reforms and reformers
World geography: continents, oceans, major physical features
Connecting geography to historical events
Economics: trade networks, industrialization, global markets
competences: Explain causes and consequences of the Civil War
Describe the impact of immigration on American society
Connect geographic features to economic and historical developments
typical_mistakes: Oversimplifying Civil War causes to a single issue
